sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Agree a new Net Change Limit for Cardano Treasury withdrawals during Epochs 613-713.
Stated amount: 500,000,000 ada net change limit; no direct withdrawal amount requested

Deliverables: Record DReps' agreement to a 500,000,000 ada cap on total Treasury Withdrawals debited during the period., Supersede any prior Net Change Limit for the same period., Apply the limit from the start of Epoch 613 through the close of Epoch 713.
Deadline/expiry: Period begins at the start of Epoch 613 (February 13, 2026) and ends at the close of Epoch 713 (on or about July 3, 2027).

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal sets a Net Change Limit of 500,000,000 ada, equal to 500,000,000,000,000 lovelace, for Cardano Treasury Withdrawals during Epochs 613-713.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposed limit caps only the total lovelace removed from the Treasury by enacted Treasury Withdrawals recorded on the Cardano ledger during the period.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, medium materiality): Treasury inflows, including reserve and protocol allocations, transaction-fee allocations, refunds, and returns of Treasury-funded amounts, do not count toward or increase the limit.
Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Treasury Withdrawals already debited during the period count toward the 500,000,000 ada cumulative limit and remain valid.
Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The Info action has no direct on-chain effect and does not authorize any Treasury Withdrawal.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal states that Yes votes exceeding 50% of active voting stake are sufficient to record DRep agreement to a Net Change Limit under TREASURY-01a.
missing: Independent evidence for: The proposal states that Yes votes exceeding 50% of active voting stake are sufficient to record DRep agreement to a Net Change Limit under TREASURY-01a.
Independent evidence for: The proposal says the existing 350,000,000 ada Net Change Limit has been useful but is becoming constrained through mid-2027.
Independent evidence for: The proposal asserts that the new 500,000,000 ada limit preserves room for DReps to consider credible Treasury Withdrawals while maintaining a fixed fiscal guardrail.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal sets a Net Change Limit of 500,000,000 ada, equal to 500,000,000,000,000 lovelace, for Cardano Treasury Withdrawals during Epochs 613-713." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The proposal states that Yes votes exceeding 50% of active voting stake are sufficient to record DRep agreement to a Net Change Limit under TREASURY-01a." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
